_meta.title: Partner Integration Lifecycle Workflow — Supplementary Content
_meta.description: Prose content extracted from Partner_Program_Dev_App_v14.docx and structured for the configurator app. Contains the six content categories not present in the structured specification: purpose, inputs, owns (scope of work), tie-breaker/escalation decision rights, outputs, explicitly does not do, failure/exception handling paths, and loop-back triggers. Keyed by step to align with structured_specification.json workflow_steps.
content_categories: purpose — one-line statement of what this step achieves
inputs — what this step receives from prior steps or external sources
owns — the scope of work performed in this step
tie_breaker_escalation — who has final authority on what, and how disputes are resolved
explicitly_does_not_do — boundaries that prevent scope creep
handoff — conditions for completion and acceptance by the next step
failure_exception_paths — non-happy-path scenarios and prescribed responses
loop_back_triggers — conditions under which work returns to a prior step
_meta.configuration_dependent_content_note: Some content within tie_breaker_escalation, failure_exception_paths, owns, and handoff varies by decision point configuration. Where applicable, these are tagged with a 'configuration_notes' object indicating which decision point answers modify the content. The structured_specification.json conditional_variations and workflow_modification_rules provide the definitive conditional logic; this file provides the base prose that those rules modify.

step_0.purpose: Define and continuously maintain the partner ecosystem operating system so execution stays repeatable as strategy, products, tooling, and partner mix evolve.
owns: Partner categories + tier definitions (eligibility, benefits, SLAs) and periodic recalibration
Default engagement motions (integration-only, referral, reseller, marketplace, etc.) and rules of engagement
Standard artifacts: intake form, triage checklist, security/legal templates, launch/support templates and their versioning
Governance: decision owners, escalation paths, operating cadences (weekly triage, monthly ops review, quarterly program review)
Tooling + data model: CRM/PRM fields, definitions, validation rules, single source of truth and ongoing data governance

Timing standards ownership: SLAs and timeboxes defined and maintained by Partnerships Ops / Partner Programs, in collaboration with functional owners (e.g., Support for support SLAs, Security/Privacy for security review SLAs, Legal for contracting timelines). Reviewed at least quarterly and after material incidents or policy changes.
Success metrics definitions and reporting standards and revisions when metrics become gameable or stale
Exception policy: when exceptions are allowed, who can approve, how long they last, how they're documented
step_0.tie_breaker_escalation_note: Step 0 is a design-time and stewardship step. It does not have case-level tie-breaker decision rights. Governance deadlocks are handled via the failure/exception paths below.
outputs: Published 'rules of the game' (current version) + templates + configured systems
Versioned change log (what changed, why, effective date) + communications to internal stakeholders
Updated governance calendar and role/accountability map (as needed)
explicitly_does_not_do: Case-by-case partner decisions (those start in Step 1)
Ad hoc exceptions outside the defined exception policy
step_0.handoff: Step 0 is considered current for execution when the latest OS version (tiers, templates, tooling, governance) is published and communicated by Partnerships Ops / Partner Programs; Step 1 is accepted by Partnerships (Partner Development / Partner Ops) using the current OS; progression is paused if the OS is under active hotfix or not yet published per Step 0 governance.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Urgent regulatory/security change or severe incident trend requires immediate program changes
failure_exception_paths.response: Invoke an 'OS Hotfix' (timeboxed interim rule/template/control with named owner + expiry date), communicate immediately, and fold into the next versioned OS release.
failure_exception_paths.likely_owner: Security/Privacy identifies → Partnerships Ops executes hotfix

failure_exception_paths.condition: CRM/PRM (or reporting pipeline) is down or data integrity is compromised
failure_exception_paths.response: Switch to the designated fallback tracker, freeze non-critical edits, assign a re-sync owner, reconcile on restoration, and publish a data correction/prevention note.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Governance repeatedly deadlocks across Product/Security/Legal/Partnerships beyond the decision SLA
failure_exception_paths.response: Escalate to the defined decision forum, force a decision within SLA, document rationale/guardrails, and update OS artifacts to prevent recurrence.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Exception requests spike ('exception debt') or cluster around the same policy area
failure_exception_paths.response: Temporarily tighten exception approvals and timebox existing exceptions, run a focused OS review, and adjust defaults (tier criteria/entitlements/standards) to eliminate repeat exceptions.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Metrics are being gamed or no longer reflect operational reality
failure_exception_paths.response: Trigger a metrics definition review, revise definitions and add counter-metrics/guardrails, update dashboards, and publish the new definitions with an effective date.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Templates/playbooks drift (multiple conflicting versions in circulation)
failure_exception_paths.response: Declare a single canonical library, deprecate duplicates, enforce versioning, and schedule a periodic audit to keep artifacts current.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Capacity changes make SLAs/tiers infeasible (support/engineering bandwidth shock)
failure_exception_paths.response: Invoke the capacity adjustment protocol, temporarily adjust SLAs/entitlements and throttle intake/prioritization, communicate changes, and update resourcing assumptions in the next OS version.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Repeated out-of-scope requests create program creep and one-off delivery/support burden
failure_exception_paths.response: Update 'supported vs not supported' boundaries, refine intake/triage criteria, add standardized reject/defer patterns and comms templates, and incorporate into the next OS release.

loop_back_triggers.trigger: Review results (re-tiering patterns, renewals/exits)
Support/incident trends (recurring failure modes)
GTM outcomes (what's working/not working in expansion motions)

primary_owner.responsibility: Owns the partner operating system (tiers, templates, tooling, governance) and versioned updates

step_1.purpose: Convert a partner request into a clean, owned work item.
owns: Capture essentials: who they are, why now, partner type hypothesis, intended motion, basic compliance flags
Quick decision: engage / decline / park
Assign internal owner(s) and log into the system

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Final call on whether to engage/decline/park a partner at intake, within the Step 0 intake criteria
May block only for hard risk constraints
Escalation target if the dispute is strategic and unresolved

outputs: Single partner record + status + owner + next-step assignment
explicitly_does_not_do: Deep technical scoping or solution design (save that for Step 3)
step_1.handoff: Step 1 is complete when Partnerships (Partner Development / Partner Ops) has created a single system-of-record entry with the minimum required intake fields and a recorded engage/decline/park decision; Step 2 is accepted by Partner Programs / Partnerships Ops for placement; progression can be blocked by Partnerships leadership (strategic no-go) or Security/Compliance (material red flags) until resolved.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Request is spam/low-quality or clearly out of scope
failure_exception_paths.response: Decline and record the reason; optionally suppress future intake from the same source per policy.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Multiple internal teams claim ownership (or duplicate records exist)
failure_exception_paths.response: Merge to a single system-of-record, assign one accountable owner, and resolve conflicts via the escalation path defined in Step 0.

failure_exception_paths.condition: High-priority partner requires expedited handling
failure_exception_paths.response: Initiate a timeboxed fast-track with minimum required intake fields; if requirements are not met within the timebox, park the request.

loop_back_triggers.trigger: Intake information is insufficient to determine engagement or routing — remain until missing information is provided (or park/decline per policy)
Recurring intake issues reveal missing fields, definitions, or routing logic — send update request to Step 0
Partner's stated motion/use case changes materially before placement — re-run Step 1 qualification and routing before proceeding

primary_owner.responsibility: Owns intake, minimum qualification, routing, and creating the system-of-record entry

contributors.responsibility: Account ownership conflicts and co-sell fit
Approve strategic go/no-go for high-impact partners

step_2.purpose: Apply the Step 0 rules to this partner.
inputs: Engaged partner record + basic context from Step 1
owns: Determine partner track + tier (based on predefined criteria)
Assign entitlements: access, SLAs, support model, co-marketing eligibility
Flag required gates (security review needed? contract needed before sandbox access?)
Set expectations for engagement model

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Final decision authority on tier placement and exceptions
Support SLAs/escalation design
Access gating
Contracting prerequisites
Motion classification and economics guardrails
Escalation target if disputes persist (via Partner Programs / Partnerships Ops)

outputs: Partner profile: tier + entitlements + required gates + engagement model
explicitly_does_not_do: Negotiate bespoke commercial terms or redesign tiers; exceptions must follow a formal exception path
step_2.handoff: Step 2 is complete when Partner Programs / Partnerships Ops assigns track/tier/entitlements and flags required gates/prerequisites in the partner profile; Step 3 is accepted by Solutions Engineering / Partner Engineering for deep scoping; progression can be blocked by unmet access prerequisites (e.g., required security/compliance gate or contracting prerequisite) per the partner profile.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Partner disputes the assigned tier/entitlements
failure_exception_paths.response: Reference the published criteria, document the decision, and route any exception request through the Step 0 exception policy (timeboxed).

failure_exception_paths.condition: Required prerequisites for access (contract, security gate, or compliance condition) are not met
failure_exception_paths.response: Limit access per policy and hold progression until satisfied; if unresolved, park/exit with rationale.
failure_exception_paths.likely_owner: Partner Programs with relevant gating function (Security/Legal/Compliance)

failure_exception_paths.condition: Motion classification is ambiguous (e.g., referral vs reseller vs integration-only) and drives conflicting expectations
failure_exception_paths.response: Escalate to Finance/RevOps and Partnerships leadership to finalize the archetype before proceeding.

loop_back_triggers.trigger: Information needed to assign tier/entitlements is missing or contradictory — return to Step 1 to collect/clarify
Partner does not fit existing tier criteria and an exception is required — escalate via Step 0 exception policy (and route any criteria/template improvements to Step 0)
Placement decisions materially change required gates or engagement model — re-validate with Step 1 inputs before proceeding

configuration_notes.DP2: Entitlements assigned in this step vary by selected commercial motions. See structured_specification.json workflow_modification_rules.step2_entitlements_by_motion for motion-specific entitlements.
configuration_notes.DP3_partner_cert: When certification requirement includes partner competency certification, certification status becomes a gating condition or entitlement in this step.
configuration_notes.DP1_no_integration_handoff: When no technical integration is selected, Step 3 (Scoping & Commitment) is skipped. Step 2 hands off directly to Step 4 (Approvals Gate).

primary_owner.responsibility: Applies tier criteria and assigns entitlements/gates consistently

step_3.step_name: Integration Request Triage, Deep Scoping, Commitment
step_3.purpose: Decide what work you'll do and what you'll commit to.
inputs: Partner profile (Step 2) + use case + initial technical context
owns: Choose integration path (certified vs supported vs partner-built)
Define scope: endpoints, auth, data flow, environments, testing approach
Identify dependencies: product gaps, security/privacy needs, legal prerequisites
Set success criteria + 'definition of done'
Make a commitment: approve / defer / reject, with owners + rough timeline

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Final decision authority on scope and roadmap commitments
Feasibility/effort estimates
Data and control constraints
Regulatory/policy constraints
tie_breaker_escalation.when_inactive: This tie-breaker authority is absent when regulated industries is not selected

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Escalation target if strategic priority conflicts with feasibility or commitments (via Solutions Engineering / Partner Engineering)

outputs: Approved integration plan/spec (scope + owners + timeline + success criteria)
explicitly_does_not_do: Execute implementation; no building here — only committed plan
step_3.handoff: Step 3 is complete when Solutions Engineering / Partner Engineering publishes an approved integration plan/spec (scope, owners, success criteria, and expected timeline) and the partner confirms intent to proceed; Step 4 is accepted by Partnerships Deal Desk / Program Manager to initiate parallel approval tracks; progression can be blocked by Product (unapproved scope/roadmap commitment) or Security/Privacy / Compliance-Risk (prohibited use/policy constraint) until re-scoped.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Partner goes dark during scoping
failure_exception_paths.response: Pause the scoping clock, timebox re-engagement, and park/close as 'Dormant' per policy; resume scoping only after re-confirming intent and context.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Critical dependencies (product/engineering/security) cannot be met in the required timeframe
failure_exception_paths.response: Defer with a revisit date and/or switch to an alternate integration path; if no viable path exists, exit with a documented rationale.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Scoping surfaces a prohibited data use or non-negotiable policy constraint
failure_exception_paths.response: Reject the request and document the reason; route any missing standards/templates to Step 0.
failure_exception_paths.likely_owner: Security/Privacy or Compliance/Risk identifies → Solutions Engineering documents

loop_back_triggers.trigger: Scoping reveals the partner's track/tier/entitlements should change — return to Step 2 for re-placement
Scoping reveals gaps in standards, templates, or integration paths — route a change request to Step 0
Requirements change materially after the plan/spec is approved — return to Step 3 before entering Step 4

configuration_notes.DP1_no_integration: This entire step is removed when integration direction = no_integration. Handoff routes directly from Step 2 to Step 4.
configuration_notes.DP1_direction: Scoping ownership and focus varies by integration direction. See structured_specification.json workflow_modification_rules.step3_scoping_ownership.
configuration_notes.DP3_neither: When certification requirement = neither, 'certified' integration path is not available. Selection is between 'supported' and 'partner-built' only.
configuration_notes.DP3_integration_cert: When certification requirement includes integration certification, 'certified' path is available and if selected, certification is a required deliverable.
configuration_notes.DP4_yes: Compliance/Risk becomes an active tie-breaker authority and can block handoff to Step 4.

primary_owner.responsibility: Owns integration path selection, deep scoping, and the committed plan/spec

contributors.responsibility: Approve product dependencies and definition of done

step_4.step_name: Approvals & Agreements Gate (Parallel Workstreams + Unblock Criteria)
step_4.purpose: Secure the required approvals and binding obligations without creating a serial bottleneck — by running reviews in parallel and defining what is needed to start implementation vs. what is needed to go live.

items: Data classification and data-flow review (what data, where it moves, retention)
Required controls (auth, encryption, access logging, incident response obligations)
Security testing requirements and evidence expectations (as applicable)
owns.configuration_note: Scope significantly reduced when no technical integration is selected (limited to data handling obligations in legal agreement only). Security testing requirements become mandatory (not 'as applicable') when certification requirement includes integration certification.

items: Regulatory/policy mapping for the use case (if regulated activity is involved)
Required operational controls, disclosures, approvals, and auditability
Risk acceptance or mitigation requirements
owns.configuration_note: This entire track is removed when regulated industries is not selected. Fully active and required when regulated industries is selected.

items: Agreement selection (template/archetype) and redline negotiation
Binding obligations captured (liability, IP, termination, SLAs, data handling)
Version control of approved language and final form routing
owns.configuration_note: Agreement type varies by commercial motions selections. Reseller agreement required when reseller motion selected. Additional marketplace platform agreement required when third-party marketplace selected.

items: Deal economics confirmation (pricing, referral/rev-share definitions, payout triggers)
Billing/invoicing/payout mechanics (including dispute handling if applicable)
Internal approvals for commercial exceptions (if allowed under Step 0 policy)
owns.configuration_note: Fully active when any commercial motion is selected (referral, reseller, marketplace, co-sell) or when co-marketing is selected (for budget mechanics). Absent when no commercial motions and no co-marketing are selected.

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Final decision authority on security controls and production risk acceptance
Regulatory/policy constraints (when applicable)
tie_breaker_escalation.when_inactive: This tie-breaker authority is absent when regulated industries is not selected

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Legal terms
Economics and payout mechanics
Escalation target if required approvals conflict or stall (via Partnerships Deal Desk)

outputs: Track-level decisions: approved / conditional approval / rejected, with documented conditions
Executed agreement(s) or approved near-final form + recorded obligations/controls
Approval record: who approved what, on which version, and what conditions remain
explicitly_does_not_do: Re-scope the integration (material scope changes route back to Step 3)
Start implementation work itself (that is Step 5), except for coordinating reviews and capturing decisions
Allow indefinite 'conditional approvals' without owners and deadlines (conditions must be tracked and time-bound)
step_4.handoff: Step 4 is complete when all preliminary approvals are obtained and all remaining approval conditions are documented with owners and deadlines by Partnerships Deal Desk / Program Manager; Step 5 is accepted by Solutions Engineering / Partner Engineering for implementation; progression can be blocked by Security/Privacy, Legal, and (where applicable) Compliance/Risk until approval conditions are satisfied.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Security/Privacy or Compliance/Risk review fails (or imposes unacceptable conditions)
failure_exception_paths.response: Return to Step 3 to redesign scope/data flows or downgrade the integration path; if not feasible, park/exit per governance.
failure_exception_paths.likely_owner: Security/Privacy or Compliance/Risk identifies → Deal Desk coordinates response

failure_exception_paths.condition: Legal negotiation stalls beyond the defined timebox or the partner insists on non-negotiable terms
failure_exception_paths.response: Escalate per Step 0 governance; if unresolved, park/exit and record the decision.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Conditional approvals are not met by the agreed deadline
failure_exception_paths.response: Pause Step 5 work, escalate owners, and either re-timebox with explicit deliverables or close the effort.

loop_back_triggers.trigger: Any track rejects or imposes scope-changing conditions — return to Step 3 (re-triage / re-scope)
Negotiation stalls past a defined threshold — escalate per Step 0 governance (or decide to park/exit)

configuration_notes.DP1_no_integration: Security & Privacy track scope significantly reduced — limited to data handling obligations in legal agreement only. No data-flow review, auth/encryption controls, or security testing. Step 5 is skipped — handoff goes to Step 6.
configuration_notes.DP2_motions: Commercial/Finance track depth varies by selected motions. Legal agreement type varies (reseller agreement, marketplace platform agreement, standard partnership). See structured_specification.json for details.
configuration_notes.DP3_integration_cert: Security testing requirements become mandatory. Specific certification standard and evidence requirements must be defined as explicit gate conditions.
configuration_notes.DP4_yes: Compliance/Risk track fully activated. Preliminary approval required before Step 5. Final sign-off required before go-live.
configuration_notes.DP1_no_integration_handoff: When no technical integration is selected, Step 5 (Implementation) is skipped. Step 4 hands off directly to Step 6 (Launch Readiness). All approval conditions must be met before proceeding to launch.

primary_owner.responsibility: Coordinates parallel approval tracks, tracks conditions, and ensures a single decision record

step_4.purpose_when_no_integration: Secure the required approvals and binding obligations by running reviews in parallel, defining what is needed before the partnership can go live.
explicitly_does_not_do_when_no_integrati: Allow indefinite 'conditional approvals' without owners and deadlines (conditions must be tracked and time-bound)
step_4.handoff_when_no_integration: Step 4 is complete when all required approvals are obtained and conditions are documented with owners and deadlines by Partnerships Deal Desk / Program Manager; Step 6 is accepted by Partnerships (Partner Enablement / Partner Ops) for launch packaging; progression can be blocked by Security/Privacy and Legal until all approval conditions are met.

step_5.purpose: Build/integrate according to the approved spec.
owns: Access provisioning, credentials, sandbox setup
Implementation and coordination across teams (partner + internal)
Joint testing, certification (if applicable), issue resolution during build
Evidence collection required for launch readiness (logs, controls, test results)

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Final authority on delivery feasibility and sequencing for disputes about scope, feasibility, or timeline during implementation
Any request that materially changes scope must return to Step 3
Escalation target if the dispute is strategic and unresolved after re-triage — for prioritization tradeoffs

outputs: Working integration meeting the spec + ready-for-launch checklist inputs
explicitly_does_not_do: Post-launch operations ownership (that starts after go-live/handoff)
step_5.handoff: Step 5 is complete when Solutions Engineering / Partner Engineering confirms the integration meets the approved spec and provides the required launch-readiness inputs (test results/evidence, known limitations, support contacts); Step 6 is accepted by Partnerships (Partner Enablement / Partner Ops) for packaging; progression can be blocked by Product (functional acceptance) or Security/Privacy (required evidence/controls not met) until resolved.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Partner goes dark mid-implementation
failure_exception_paths.response: Pause work, stop internal effort after timeboxed follow-ups, and close as 'Dormant'; require re-confirmation of scope via Step 3 before resuming.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Implementation reveals blockers that invalidate the committed plan (access, environments, repeated test failures)
failure_exception_paths.response: Escalate per governance; return to Step 3 for re-scope and/or Step 4 for revised conditions as needed.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Build outcomes cannot satisfy Step 4 production conditions
failure_exception_paths.response: Do not proceed to launch; remain in Step 5 until conditions are met or formally de-scope/exit.

loop_back_triggers.trigger: Implementation uncovers scope gaps or new requirements — return to Step 3 (re-triage / re-scope)
Implementation cannot satisfy Step 4 approval conditions or requires different controls — return to Step 4 for updated review/conditions
Repeated implementation blockers indicate missing standards or templates — route an improvement request to Step 0

configuration_notes.DP1_no_integration: This entire step is removed when integration direction = no_integration. Handoff routes directly from Step 4 to Step 6.
configuration_notes.DP1_direction: Implementation ownership varies by integration direction. See structured_specification.json workflow_modification_rules.step5_implementation_ownership.
configuration_notes.DP3_integration_cert: Certification becomes a required deliverable before handoff to Step 6. 'Joint testing, certification' is a mandatory sub-item, not conditional. Evidence collection must include certification artifacts.

primary_owner.responsibility: Leads implementation coordination and acceptance against the approved spec

entry_triggers.description: Conditions required before implementation may begin (set during Step 4 approvals).
gates: Security/Privacy provides preliminary approval for non-production work (e.g., sandbox/dev) or explicitly states acceptable scope for implementation work to proceed
Legal provides an approved agreement path (e.g., template selected + redline boundaries / fallback positions agreed)
Any hard 'no-go' risks have been resolved (e.g., prohibited data use, unacceptable control gaps)
entry_triggers.when_DP4_yes: If Compliance/Risk is required for the use case, include either preliminary approval or a defined set of constraints that implementation can proceed under.
entry_triggers.when_DP1_no_integration: Step 5 is skipped entirely when no technical integration is selected — these conditions apply to proceeding to Step 6 instead.

step_6.purpose: Make the integration 'real' for users and internal teams.
owns: Partner-facing launch kit: docs, integration guide, known limitations, support contacts
Internal enablement: support playbook, escalation procedures, sales/CS notes
Marketplace/listing readiness (if applicable): metadata, positioning, compatibility notes
Launch comms plan + approvals (brand/legal/compliance as needed)

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Final authority on what can be approved/published for disputes on launch readiness or public-facing assets (docs, listing copy, announcements)
Final authority on technical/product claims
Owns channel/timing once approvals are secured
Escalation target if stakeholders disagree on timing tradeoffs after approvals

outputs: Launch package + enablement artifacts + comms/listing readiness
explicitly_does_not_do: Major engineering changes (those go back to Step 5 / Step 3 if scope changes)
step_6.handoff: Step 6 is complete when Partnerships (Partner Enablement / Partner Ops) confirms launch artifacts are complete and required approvals for partner-facing/public assets are obtained; Step 7 is accepted by Engineering (integration owner / SRE-on-point) to schedule cutover; progression can be blocked by Engineering (readiness/cutover prerequisites unmet) or Security/Privacy / Compliance-Risk / Legal (production go-live prerequisites not satisfied) until cleared.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Legal/compliance/brand approval blocks public-facing assets or announcements
failure_exception_paths.response: Timebox revisions, remove/adjust claims as required, and postpone launch communications if approvals cannot be secured.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Documentation, runbooks, or internal enablement are incomplete
failure_exception_paths.response: Do not proceed to Step 7; complete Step 6 deliverables or route necessary fixes to Step 5 (and Step 4 if new obligations emerge).

failure_exception_paths.condition: Marketplace/listing requirements are rejected or cannot be met in time
failure_exception_paths.response: Proceed without the listing (if acceptable) or postpone go-live until listing/readiness criteria are satisfied, per governance.

loop_back_triggers.trigger: Launch readiness identifies missing approvals/evidence or unresolved obligations — return to Step 4

loop_back_triggers.target: Step 5 (or Step 3 if scope materially changes)
loop_back_triggers.trigger: Launch readiness requires technical fixes or additional testing — return to Step 5
Documentation/enablement gaps are systemic — route updates to Step 0 for versioned improvements

configuration_notes.DP1_no_integration: Launch kit covers commercial motion only — referral submission process, reseller ordering, marketplace listing usage. No integration guide or technical documentation.
configuration_notes.DP1_has_integration: Launch kit includes integration guide, technical documentation, and known technical limitations in addition to commercial content.
configuration_notes.DP2_marketplace: Marketplace/listing readiness items are active when any marketplace motion is selected.
configuration_notes.DP2_co_marketing: Launch comms plan and brand/legal/compliance approvals are required (not optional) when co-marketing is active.
configuration_notes.DP3_partner_cert: Partner-facing enablement packaging must include certification program materials: training content, assessment details, certification pathway, and badge/credential information.
configuration_notes.DP3_integration_cert: Launch readiness checklist must include certification evidence as a confirmed item.

primary_owner.responsibility: Owns launch packaging, enablement coordination, and readiness artifacts

entry_triggers.description: Conditions required before production go-live and launch packaging may begin (set during Step 4 approvals).
gates: Final Security/Privacy sign-off (production scope) and required evidence completed
Executed legal agreement(s) (or documented executive exception per Step 0 policy)
Confirmed commercial/finance mechanics (including attribution/payout setup if relevant)
entry_triggers.when_DP4_yes: Final Compliance/Risk sign-off is also required.
entry_triggers.when_DP4_no: Compliance/Risk sign-off is absent from go-live criteria.

step_6.purpose_when_no_integration: Make the partnership operational for users and internal teams.
inputs_when_no_integration: Approved agreements, cleared gates, and contractual/support obligations (Step 4)
explicitly_does_not_do_when_no_integrati: Negotiate bespoke terms outside the approved agreements (those route back to Step 4)

step_7.purpose: Execute production launch, stabilize, and confirm operational readiness.
owns: Go-live decision and cutover
Hypercare window: monitoring, rapid triage, rollback plan readiness
Confirm escalation works (who responds, in what time)
Formal handoff confirmation from build team to ops/support ownership

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Final authority on technical cutover readiness and rollback
Final authority on production control readiness
Final authority on product readiness/feature scope
Escalation target if a go-live decision is disputed after mandatory approvals

outputs: Live integration + stabilized baseline + confirmed operational handoff
explicitly_does_not_do: Long-term support and continuous improvement (that's Step 8)
step_7.handoff: Step 7 is complete when Engineering (integration owner / SRE-on-point) confirms stabilization/hypercare exit criteria are met and the operational handoff package is complete; Step 8 is accepted by Support / Operations as the new owner of steady-state SLAs and incident response; progression can be blocked if Support / Operations does not accept the handoff due to missing runbooks, contacts, or unresolved critical issues.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Go-live is rolled back
failure_exception_paths.response: Execute the rollback plan, open an incident, extend hypercare, return to Step 5 for fixes, and refresh Step 6 readiness artifacts before re-attempting Step 7.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Sev1/critical incident occurs during hypercare
failure_exception_paths.response: Activate incident response, freeze further rollout, and exit hypercare only after stability criteria are met; then proceed to Step 8.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Partner cutover activities are incomplete or readiness is not met at launch time
failure_exception_paths.response: Postpone the launch and return to Step 6 (and Step 5 as needed) before re-scheduling Step 7.
failure_exception_paths.likely_owner: Engineering coordinates with partner via Solutions Engineering

loop_back_triggers.trigger: Go-live readiness conditions are not met at cutover — return to Step 6 (readiness packaging) and/or Step 5 (implementation fixes) as needed

loop_back_triggers.target: Step 5 (or Step 3 if scope materially changes)
loop_back_triggers.trigger: Stabilization/hypercare uncovers issues requiring material technical changes — return to Step 5
Rollback or launch delay materially changes readiness artifacts or obligations — re-run Step 6 before re-attempting Step 7

configuration_notes.DP1_no_integration: No technical cutover. No rollback plan. Hypercare is limited to monitoring commercial motion activation (e.g., first referral submitted, first listing live) rather than technical stability.

primary_owner.responsibility: Owns production cutover, monitoring, rollback readiness, and stabilization

contributors.responsibility: Final go/no-go from a product perspective
Confirm control readiness and incident obligations

inputs_when_no_integration: Approved agreements and confirmed commercial mechanics (Step 4)

step_8.purpose: Keep integrations healthy and reduce recurring friction over time.
owns: Ticket triage, incident management, escalation per SLA
Health metrics: uptime, error rates, volume, latency (as applicable)
Root cause + prevention loops: docs updates, tooling fixes, backlog creation
Operational reporting: support burden, partner health, reliability trends

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Final decision authority on incident severity, communications, and SLA enforcement
Technical remediation path and deployment readiness
Security incidents and production risk response
Prioritization tradeoffs
If disputes impact partner entitlements or whether to pause Step 9 growth motions — escalate for resolution and log for Step 10 review

outputs: Stable operations + improved docs/tooling + prioritized improvement backlog
explicitly_does_not_do: Growth motions or commercial expansion unless explicitly triggered (Step 9)
step_8.handoff: Step 8 is complete (for growth activation) when Support / Operations confirms the integration is stable and the agreed activation signals are met; Step 9 is accepted by Partnerships (Partner GTM / Strategic Partnerships) to initiate growth plays; progression can be blocked by Support / Operations if stability/support-burden thresholds are not met or by RevOps/Finance if attribution/payout mechanics are not ready where required.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Repeated incidents cross a defined threshold
failure_exception_paths.response: Initiate a root-cause program; route systemic standards/template updates to Step 0; if redesign is required, return to Step 3 (then Steps 4-7 as needed).

failure_exception_paths.condition: SLA breaches persist or support burden becomes unsustainable
failure_exception_paths.response: Trigger a remediation plan and/or a re-tier recommendation for Step 10; pause Step 9 growth motions if required to protect reliability.
failure_exception_paths.likely_owner: Support/Operations with Partnerships for re-tier recommendation

failure_exception_paths.condition: Partner repeatedly fails to follow required support inputs/processes
failure_exception_paths.response: Enforce the playbook, escalate via governance, and address behavior/terms in Step 10 (and Step 0 if standards need updating).

loop_back_triggers.trigger: Recurring incidents/failure modes indicate missing program standards, templates, or SLAs — route improvements to Step 0
Operational issues require material technical changes beyond routine fixes — return to Step 3
Partner is stable and meets growth activation signals — proceed to Step 9

configuration_notes.DP1_no_integration: Health metrics focus on commercial indicators — referral quality, pipeline accuracy, listing performance, support burden. No uptime, error rates, or latency to monitor.
configuration_notes.DP1_has_integration: Full health metrics active including uptime, error rates, volume, and latency.

primary_owner.responsibility: Owns steady-state SLAs, ticket triage, and incident response

contributors.responsibility: Security incidents and response coordination where applicable

step_9.step_name: Value Realization: Triggered Growth Motions (Co-sell / Co-marketing / Expansion)
step_9.purpose: Convert a stable integration and healthy partner relationship into measurable business outcomes using repeatable growth plays, clear triggers, and auditable attribution/economics.
inputs: Partner tier/entitlements (Step 2) and any commercial terms (Step 4)
Current performance data: adoption, pipeline, revenue, support burden, segment coverage

entry_triggers.description: Step 9 starts when at least one of the following is true (and Step 8 indicates acceptable stability)
gates: Stability gate: integration has met baseline reliability/support thresholds over a defined period
Adoption gate: usage crosses a defined threshold or shows sustained growth
Revenue/pipeline gate: partner-sourced or partner-influenced pipeline exceeds a threshold
Strategic gate: partner unlocks a priority segment/geo/vertical or a platform distribution channel
Efficiency gate: support burden is within acceptable limits (or improving) such that scaling won't create operational debt
RevOps/Finance confirms attribution and payout mechanics are ready
entry_triggers.governance_note: Activation thresholds and periods are defined and maintained by Partnerships (Partner GTM / Strategic Partnerships) in collaboration with Support/Operations and RevOps/Finance, and documented in the Partner Operating System (Step 0); reviewed at least quarterly and after material incidents or strategic changes.

items: Account mapping and lead-sharing rules
Joint pitch narrative, pricing positioning, objection handling
Opportunity stages, required artifacts, handoff rules between teams
Weekly pipeline review cadence and close-plan ownership
Campaign type selection (webinar, content, event, community, partner newsletter)
Asset creation + approvals workflow (brand/legal/compliance if needed)
Lead capture + routing rules and follow-up SLA
Post-campaign performance review and learnings
Listing quality standards (positioning, screenshots, compatibility, known limits)
Release notes / feature announcements
Promotion schedule and performance tracking (views → installs → active usage)
New use case activation (additional workflows, vertical templates)
Deeper integration depth (additional endpoints/modules)
New segment rollout (new geography, new customer cohort)
Enablement refresh (partner team + internal sales/CS/support)
Credit definitions: sourced vs influenced vs assisted (and what evidence qualifies)
Timestamp rules: when credit is assigned and how it expires
System fields: required CRM/PRM fields + validation (single source of truth)
Payout triggers: what event triggers payment (e.g., contract signed, invoice paid)
Payout schedule + reconciliation: cadence, reporting format, dispute process, and approvers
Exception handling: how disputes are escalated and resolved
owns.active_when: Any commercial motion selected (referral, reseller, marketplace, co-sell)

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Final decision authority on attribution, payout eligibility, and reconciliation
Final authority on account ownership rules for co-sell motions
Final authority on claims/approvals for public-facing activities
Escalation target if disputes persist (via Partnerships / Partner GTM / Strategic Partnerships)

outputs: A partner growth plan (plays selected, owners, timeline, targets)
Execution artifacts (enablement deck, campaign assets, listing updates, account map)
KPI tracking: pipeline created/influenced, conversion, adoption/activation, attach rate, support burden impact
Post-play review: what worked/what didn't + recommendations routed to Step 0 (program improvements)
explicitly_does_not_do: Run growth motions before the integration is operationally stable (that remains Step 8/7 gating)
Change core integration scope without re-entering Step 3 (expansion that materially changes scope triggers Step 3)
Redefine attribution rules ad hoc outside the approved program definitions (Step 0 governs definitions; Step 9 executes them)
step_9.handoff: Step 9 is complete when Partnerships (Partner GTM / Strategic Partnerships) records executed plays, outcomes, and any re-tier/remediation recommendations; Step 10 is accepted by Partnerships (Partner Management) for QBR/annual review; progression may be delayed if required performance, economics, or risk inputs from RevOps/Finance, Support/Operations, or Security/Compliance are not available.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Attribution/credit or payout eligibility is disputed
failure_exception_paths.response: Follow the defined dispute process, freeze credit/payouts for the contested items, and resolve via RevOps/Finance escalation with documented outcome.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Co-sell motions create account ownership conflict or partner-led deals bypass agreed rules
failure_exception_paths.response: Pause the specific play, resolve ownership per sales governance, and restart only with clarified rules and logging requirements.
failure_exception_paths.likely_owner: Sales governance resolves → Partnerships (Partner GTM) restarts play

failure_exception_paths.condition: Growth motions repeatedly fail to produce measurable outcomes or the partner under-delivers on commitments
failure_exception_paths.response: Downgrade/adjust the play mix, reset targets, and log a re-tier or remediation recommendation for Step 10.

loop_back_triggers.trigger: Scaling increases incidents/support burden beyond threshold — return to Step 8 (stabilize)
Expansion requires material technical changes — return to Step 3 (re-triage/scoping)

configuration_notes.DP1_no_integration: The expansion play's integration-deepening activities are removed when no technical integration is selected. The enablement refresh activities include certification renewal when partner competency certification is required.
configuration_notes.DP2: Each selected motion activates its corresponding play and items. Non-selected motions are entirely absent.
configuration_notes.DP2_co_sell_direction: Account ownership rules and credit definitions vary by co-sell direction. See structured_specification.json workflow_modification_rules.step9_co_sell_account_ownership.
configuration_notes.DP2_referral_direction: When both referral_inbound and referral_outbound are selected, attribution mechanics must distinguish between the two flows. See structured_specification.json workflow_modification_rules.step9_referral_attribution_direction.
configuration_notes.DP3_partner_cert: Enablement refresh (expansion play) explicitly includes certification renewal or update for partner team and internal sales/CS/support.

primary_owner.responsibility: Owns selecting and executing growth plays with the partner and tracking outcomes

contributors.responsibility: Approvals for co-marketing or commercial amendments where needed

step_10.step_name: Periodic Review: Renew / Re-tier / Exit (and Program Feedback)
step_10.purpose: Make intentional lifecycle decisions for each partner and feed learnings back into the partner operating system.
owns: QBR/annual review outcomes: invest / maintain / remediate / sunset
Re-tiering decisions and entitlement adjustments (per Step 0 criteria)
Renewal, amendment, termination decisions (and who needs to approve)
Exit planning: deprecation timeline, migrations, comms, and data retention obligations
Identification of systemic issues and improvement opportunities (process, tooling, standards)

tie_breaker_escalation.scope: Final decision authority on unsustainable operational risk and required remediation before renewal
Unacceptable risk posture
Economics viability
Contractual constraints
Escalation target if stakeholders disagree on renew/re-tier/exit outcomes — final decision within the decision SLA

outputs: Decision record per partner: renew / re-tier / remediate plan / exit (with rationale and owners)
Updated commercial/legal actions: renewal paperwork, amendments, termination notices (as applicable)
Exit or remediation plan with milestones and communications plan

Program improvement recommendations / change requests routed to Step 0 (e.g., tier criteria updates, template updates, SLA/escalation changes, intake/triage refinements, tooling/data definition changes)
explicitly_does_not_do: Implement operating system changes directly (those are owned and versioned in Step 0)
Re-scope major integration work ad hoc; material changes route back to Step 3
step_10.handoff_note: Step 10 is the lifecycle review step. Its outputs route to Step 0 (program improvements), Step 3 (re-scope if needed), or result in renewal/exit decisions. There is no single 'next step' — the outcome determines the routing.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Renewal or amendment negotiations stall beyond the agreed timebox
failure_exception_paths.response: Escalate per governance, proceed with a default decision path (renew/exit) as per policy, and begin contingency planning for exit.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Exit decision creates material customer or operational risk
failure_exception_paths.response: Implement a timeboxed remediation or migration plan before termination; coordinate customer communications and obligations before executing exit.

failure_exception_paths.condition: Partner contests review outcomes (re-tier, reduced entitlements, exit)
failure_exception_paths.response: Document the rationale, offer a remediation path with measurable criteria and deadlines, and re-evaluate only upon completion.
failure_exception_paths.likely_owner: Partnerships (Partner Management) documents and offers remediation path

loop_back_triggers.trigger: Remediation requires product/integration changes — return to Step 3 (re-triage/scoping)
Recurring operational failures are identified — send improvement backlog to Step 0 for versioned updates

configuration_notes.DP2_financial_motion: Finance/RevOps has final decision authority on economics viability at renewal/re-tier/exit review when commercial motions are selected.
configuration_notes.DP2_no_financial_motion: Finance/RevOps authority at Step 10 is limited to general commercial constraints — no payout economics to adjudicate.

primary_owner.responsibility: Owns QBR/annual reviews and lifecycle decisions (renew, re-tier, remediate, exit)
